mapper.sql: project_access as ( select p.project_id, p.project_name, p.owner_id from project p left join ( select pm.project_id, count(*) as member_count from project_member pm join team_member tm on pm.team_member_id = tm.team_member_id where pm.project_id = #{projectId} and tm.user_id = #{userId} group by pm.project_id ) user_access on p.project_id = user_access.project_id where p.project_id = #{projectId} and ( p.owner_id = #{userId} or nvl(user_access.member_count, 0) > 0 ) )

user_permission as ( select nvl(max(case when perm.permission_code = #{updatePermissionCode} then 1 else 0 end), 0) as all_update_count, nvl(max(case when perm.permission_code = #{updateOwnPermissionCode} then 1 else 0 end), 0) as own_update_count from project_member pm join team_member tm on pm.team_member_id = tm.team_member_id left join role_permission rp on pm.role_id = rp.role_id left join permission perm on rp.permission_id = perm.permission_id where pm.project_id = #{projectId} and tm.user_id = #{userId} )
mapper.select:
with , , issue_page as ( select * from ( select issue_rows.*, rownum as row_sort from ( select /*+ index(i UK_ISSUE_PROJECT_NO) */ i.issue_id, i.issue_no, '#' || lpad(i.issue_no, 4, '0') as display_issue_no, i.title as issue_title, i.issue_status_id, ist.status_name as issue_status_name, ist.closed_yn, i.assignee_id, assignee.user_name as assignee_name from issue i join project_access p on i.project_id = p.project_id cross join user_permission up left join issue_status ist on i.issue_status_id = ist.issue_status_id left join users assignee on i.assignee_id = assignee.user_id where ( p.owner_id = #{userId} or up.all_update_count > 0 or ( i.assignee_id = #{userId} and up.own_update_count > 0 ) )
and ( lower(i.title) like '%' || lower(#{issueSearchKeyword}) || '%' or to_char(i.issue_no) like '%' || #{issueSearchKeyword} || '%' )
order by i.issue_no desc ) issue_rows where rownum <= 50 ) ),
selected_issue as ( select /*+ index(i PK_ISSUE) */ i.issue_id, i.issue_no, '#' || lpad(i.issue_no, 4, '0') as display_issue_no, i.title as issue_title, i.issue_status_id, ist.status_name as issue_status_name, ist.closed_yn, i.assignee_id, assignee.user_name as assignee_name, 0 as row_sort from issue i join project_access p on i.project_id = p.project_id cross join user_permission up left join issue_status ist on i.issue_status_id = ist.issue_status_id left join users assignee on i.assignee_id = assignee.user_id where i.issue_id = #{selectedIssueId} and ( p.owner_id = #{userId} or up.all_update_count > 0 or ( i.assignee_id = #{userId} and up.own_update_count > 0 ) ) and not exists ( select 1 from issue_page where issue_page.issue_id = i.issue_id ) ),
issue_limited as ( select * from issue_page
union all select * from selected_issue
) select 'PROJECT' as option_group, 0 as option_sort, 1 as row_sort, p.project_id, p.project_name, p.owner_id, null as setting_code_id, null as work_type_name, null as issue_id, null as issue_no, null as display_issue_no, null as issue_title, null as issue_status_name, null as closed_yn, null as assignee_id, null as assignee_name from project_access p
union all
select 'WORK_TYPE' as option_group, 1 as option_sort, row_number() over (order by sc.default_yn desc, sc.setting_code_id asc) as row_sort, p.project_id, p.project_name, p.owner_id, sc.setting_code_id, sc.setting_name as work_type_name, null as issue_id, null as issue_no, null as display_issue_no, null as issue_title, null as issue_status_name, null as closed_yn, null as assignee_id, null as assignee_name from project_access p join setting_code sc on sc.user_id = p.owner_id where sc.setting_group_name = 'g001'
union all
select 'ISSUE' as option_group, 2 as option_sort, issue_limited.row_sort, p.project_id, p.project_name, p.owner_id, null as setting_code_id, null as work_type_name, issue_limited.issue_id, issue_limited.issue_no, issue_limited.display_issue_no, issue_limited.issue_title, issue_limited.issue_status_name, issue_limited.closed_yn, issue_limited.assignee_id, issue_limited.assignee_name from issue_limited cross join project_access p order by option_sort, row_sort

with ,
select p.project_id, p.project_name, p.owner_id, ( select count(*) from issue i cross join user_permission up where i.project_id = p.project_id and i.issue_id = #{issueId} and ( p.owner_id = #{userId} or up.all_update_count > 0 or ( i.assignee_id = #{userId} and up.own_update_count > 0 ) ) ) as issue_access_count, ( select count(*) from setting_code sc where sc.user_id = p.owner_id and sc.setting_group_name = 'g001' and sc.setting_code_id = #{settingCodeId} ) as work_type_count from project_access p
mapper.insert: insert into time_log ( time_log_id, issue_id, user_id, setting_code_id, work_date, hours, description, created_at ) values ( #{timeLogId}, #{issueId}, #{userId}, #{settingCodeId}, #{workDate}, #{hours}, #{description}, sysdate )
mapper.select:
with ,
select tl.time_log_id, tl.issue_id, tl.user_id, tl.setting_code_id, tl.work_date, tl.hours, tl.description, case when length(tl.description) > 80 then substr(tl.description, 1, 80) || '...' else tl.description end as description_preview, tl.created_at, p.project_id, p.project_name, p.owner_id, i.issue_no, '#' || lpad(i.issue_no, 4, '0') as display_issue_no, i.title as issue_title, ist.status_name as issue_status_name, ist.closed_yn, i.assignee_id, assignee.user_name as assignee_name, writer.user_name, sc.setting_name as work_type_name, case when p.owner_id = #{userId} then 'Y' when up.all_update_count > 0 then 'Y' when i.assignee_id = #{userId} and up.own_update_count > 0 then 'Y' else 'N' end as update_permission_yn from time_log tl join issue i on tl.issue_id = i.issue_id join project_access p on i.project_id = p.project_id cross join user_permission up left join issue_status ist on i.issue_status_id = ist.issue_status_id left join users assignee on i.assignee_id = assignee.user_id left join users writer on tl.user_id = writer.user_id left join setting_code sc on tl.setting_code_id = sc.setting_code_id where tl.time_log_id = #{timeLogId} and tl.issue_id = #{issueId}
mapper.update: update time_log set issue_id = #{issueId}, setting_code_id = #{settingCodeId}, work_date = #{workDate}, hours = #{hours}, description = #{description} where time_log_id = #{timeLogId}
mapper.select: select * from ( select tl.time_log_id, tl.issue_id, tl.user_id, writer.user_name, tl.setting_code_id, sc.setting_name as work_type_name, tl.work_date, tl.hours, tl.description, case when length(tl.description) > 70 then substr(tl.description, 1, 70) || '...' else tl.description end as description_preview, tl.created_at from time_log tl join issue i on tl.issue_id = i.issue_id join project p on i.project_id = p.project_id left join users writer on tl.user_id = writer.user_id left join setting_code sc on tl.setting_code_id = sc.setting_code_id where tl.issue_id = #{issueId} and i.project_id = #{projectId} and ( p.owner_id = #{userId} or exists ( select 1 from project_member pm join team_member tm on pm.team_member_id = tm.team_member_id where pm.project_id = p.project_id and tm.user_id = #{userId} ) ) order by tl.work_date desc, tl.created_at desc, tl.time_log_id desc ) where rownum <= 5
